steps.title: Find the needed boundary
steps.content: The first clause is complete once the unusual orange sand dazzles visitors. The next idea begins with a dependent When clause and then explains that taking sand home disturbs the ecosystem.

steps.title: Reject fused continuations
steps.content: Choice A and choice C blur the boundary between the dazzling sand and the later consequence of visitors taking sand. Choice B creates faulty coordination with and when. A period after visitors cleanly separates the two sentence units. The answer is D.
